lane: Lane 1 v4(b) — 2D cache blocking, DE-RISK probe (not built; informs the build/skip decision)
question: Is the owner memory-bound on input re-streaming (-> 2D cache blocking would pay), or compute/bit-exact-tax-bound (-> dead end)?
method: camelid bench-owner-sweep (hardened, in-process paired, rounds=4) on Llama-3.2-3B-Q8 at 3 prompt lengths. Read owner_vnni4x8 prefill tok/s vs length + the owner/off ratio (the ratio cancels attention's O(M^2) growth, isolating the matmul path 2D blocking targets).
findings: Owner IS memory-bound: vnni4x8 prefill tok/s falls 30.28 -> 27.44 -> 23.31 (-23%) as the prompt grows 393 -> 1473. NOT a dead end.

Owner ADVANTAGE erodes with length: owner/off 1.139 -> 1.130 -> 1.106. As the workload becomes more memory-bound, the owner's compute edge (VNNI + weight residency) matters less and converges toward the default.
The eroding ratio is the 2D-blocking-recoverable part (attention O(M^2) hits both configs and cancels in the ratio).
verdict: REAL BUT MODEST, CONTEXT-SCALING. 2D cache blocking would recover the owner's advantage-erosion: ~+3% at ~1.5K tokens, growing at longer contexts (4K/8K erode further -> bigger recovery there). For headline (<=1.5K-token) prefill the ROI (~3%) does NOT justify the highest-complexity kernel change in the stack (esp. after v4(a) regressed against intuition). Absolute ceiling still capped by the bit-exact tax.
decision: DEFERRED. Build 2D blocking ONLY if long-context prefill becomes a priority (where it pays most). Otherwise the owner at +13.5%/+15.8% (0.78x->0.89x / 0.67x->0.81x of llama, bit-exact) stands. The actual unlock to SHIP this (promote to default) is the Ubuntu/second host (both-host treaty), which Tim is provisioning.
